node.title: What Am I Doing Wrong? Need Adivice

article.title: What Am I Doing Wrong? Need Adivice
article.content:
I've been actively applying to Google, Databricks, Uber, Meta, Rubrik, DE Shaw, etc. through their careers website for over a year now, but I keep getting rejected every time. Also, I haven’t received any outreach from any recruiters on LinkedIn.

Here’s a quick summary of my background:
2023 graduate, 5★ on CodeChef, Expert on Codeforces, Guardian on LeetCode
Consistently Leetcoding

I also switched my resume from a two-column format to a one-column format, thinking it might help(saw one post from a person who interviewd for Google), but that didn’t make a difference.

I'm starting to wonder if there's something fundamentally wrong with my application strategy or resume. Any advice or insights would be really appreciated!

post.content: It\u2019s not about your resume; it\u2019s just that your right time hasn\u2019t come yet.\n

1. In general most resume advice given by ENGINEERS is not good as engineers don't screen the resume. Recruiters are not technical people , they can only look at buzzwords and previous company name, optimize for that. And of course if you can optimize your resume for a particular role that might help. 

2.  referral / directly connecting with recruiters >>>>>> applying on career websites . 

For the bigger compaines they get A LOT OF applications on career sites and it is difficult to get noticed there, referrals can get you noticed easier and directly messaging recruiters / hiring team or manager has best chance. Also people in bigger companies get a lot of such messages so it is difficult to get their attention but this strategy works very well for smaller companies.

Nothing wrong bro have patience i am in the same boat and having similar stats as yours , you can do one thing like instead of applying directly reach out to recruiters cold mail them get email id , and stick to the things what you are doing

Directly applying through their careers website is rarely successful. A better approach would be to apply via referrals. Also, I\'m not sure if anybody cares about codechef/leetcode rating. \nFrom what I\'ve heard, market is not great for candidates with less than 2-3 years of experience for quite some time now...
Start applying on Naukri and Indeed
Instead of waiting, why don\'t you reach out to recruiters through LinkedIn?
Directly Apply it through the career site also you can reach technical recruiter directly through linked IN
